key_facts: Japan's Imperial Household Law restricts succession to male-line males, creating a thin succession line (only Prince Hisahito after Crown Prince Akishino)
A 2021-2022 government advisory panel recommended allowing female members to retain royal status after marriage and adopting male-line males from former imperial branches
The advisory panel did NOT recommend female-line succession or a reigning empress
Japan's ordinary Diet sessions typically run 150 days from January through June
No imperial succession reform legislation had been enacted as of early 2025 despite years of discussion
Japanese public polling has consistently shown majority support (typically 70-80%) for allowing a female emperor
CRITICAL CAVEAT: All web search tools were unavailable; these findings could not be verified against current May 2026 sources
